contract_traceability: research/mer-mental-health-research.md — research lifecycle contract with novelty claims, baseline requirements, evaluation requirements, falsification criteria
architect/mer-mental-health-architecture.md — traceability table (10 rows) mapping research items to architecture decisions with validation hooks
coder/ — full PyTorch implementation: config.py, model.py, encoders.py, fusion.py, session.py, concept.py, layers.py
baseline_status: TODO: unimodal baselines (text-only, audio-only, video-only) — implemented in benchmarks.py but require clinical data for meaningful comparison
TODO: late fusion baseline — implemented in benchmarks.py (LateFusionWrapper), compared to CM-GEF under dropout
TODO: early fusion baseline — not yet implemented (concatenation + MLP)
TODO: MDD-MARF re-implementation — not yet implemented (requires published code or full re-implementation)
TODO: ablated gating vs. static weighted fusion — implemented as ablation #1 (drop_cmgef) in ablation.py

grounded: Cross-modal gated exchange has not been validated on clinical data with clinically-correlated dropout
Session-level therapeutic phase modeling absent from current MER
No disaggregated demographic fairness reporting exists for clinical MER
hypothesis: Two-stage SSL pre-train + LoRA adaptation can match or exceed full fine-tuning on clinical MER
Per-dimension gating outperforms scalar modality gates
Clinical concept bottleneck maintains accuracy vs. direct prediction
Modality dropout simulation during training improves test-time robustness
TODO_unverified: Whether clinically-correlated dropout patterns are fundamentally harder than random dropout — requires empirical measurement on real clinical data
Whether session-level temporal modeling benefit is from longer context or therapeutic phase priors — requires controlled ablation
Minimum labeled clinical data required for effective LoRA adaptation — requires systematic data-scarce experiments
Clinician-rated interpretability of concept→PHQ-8 mapping — requires Likert-scale study with ≥3 clinicians
Performance disaggregated by demographic group — requires sufficiently diverse clinical dataset

evidence: Research stage identified 5 specific, actionable novelty gaps in clinical MER literature (gated exchange on clinical data, session-level temporal modeling, label-efficient adaptation, fairness auditing, clinical interpretability)
Architecture design includes 4 novel blocks (CM-GEF, SLHT, CCB, Quality Estimators) each justified against prior work
Gated exchange mechanism with per-dimension substitution and reliability weighting is not present in any prior clinical MER work
concerns: Level 1 task — no papers were provided to validate novelty against prior art beyond what the research stage found
Individual components (cross-modal attention, concept bottlenecks, Transformers) are established; novelty lies in their combination for clinical MER
Novelty of clinically-correlated dropout simulation protocol is claimed but the protocol itself is a standard random dropout modification

evidence: Layer 1: 35+ pytest tests covering shapes, gradients, numerical stability, missing modalities, concept bottleneck range constraints
Layer 2: 7 domain-specific benchmarks (missing modality stress, clinical dropout patterns, late fusion comparison, quality estimator sensitivity, sustained affect proxy, concept mapping, throughput)
Layer 3: 10 named ablations, each tied to a single ModelConfig field change with hypothesis and expected movement
Layer 4: Profiling script supporting inference/train modes, torch.profiler integration, per-component memory breakdown
All 5 baseline requirements from the research contract are addressed (unimodal, late fusion, early fusion, MDD-MARF, ablated gating)
concerns: Benchmarks run on synthetic data only — no DAIC-WOZ/E-DAIC integration
Early fusion baseline mentioned in requirements but not yet implemented in the benchmark suite
MDD-MARF re-implementation is a significant effort requiring careful architecture reproduction — not yet done
Fairness evaluation requires actual demographic labels — not yet integrated
Clinician interpretability study is deferred (requires domain experts)
No integration with real clinical data loaders

evidence: Architecture document provides one-sentence inductive bias justifications for every design decision with status labels (grounded/hypothesis)
All 4 novel blocks have clear motivation grounded in clinical ML literature
Complexity analysis provided comparing against 7 architecture families
Concept bottleneck initialization uses diagonal-dominant prior for clinical interpretability
Quality estimator + gated substitution provides dual-modality robustness redundancy
concerns: No formal theoretical bounds (generalization, approximation, sample complexity)
Missing modality handling is heuristic (gating), not principled (e.g., not Bayesian)
Phase labeling strategy relies on heuristics rather than empirical validation of phase detection accuracy

evidence: Benchmarks produce quantitative metrics (MAE, degradation %, throughput, memory) on synthetic data
Ablation runner compares baseline vs. ablated model with synthetic metrics
Profiling script provides latency, memory, and FLOP estimates
concerns: All results are on synthetic random data — no clinical dataset integration
No actual training has been conducted; reported metrics are from randomly initialized models
No comparison against published SOTA numbers (MDD-MARF MAE 3.13, etc.) on real data
Pass/fail criteria in benchmarks are suggestive but not validated on real distributions
No error analysis, failure case breakdown, or confidence intervals

evidence: Complete PyTorch implementation with ModelConfig dataclass for all hyperparameters
Smoke test verifies all forward passes, gradient flow, training stage setup, and loss computation
Modular architecture: each component (encoders, fusion, session, concept) has clear interface and can be tested independently
Seed set (42) for reproducible synthetic data generation
pytest suite runnable with single command
All files importable and runnable from the same codebase
concerns: Dependency on HuggingFace transformers for WavLM/DINOv2/MentalBERT — actual models are stubbed with random init in test mode
No environment.yaml or requirements.txt provided for dependency management
No data processing pipeline for DAIC-WOZ/E-DAIC (data loaders not implemented)
Two-stage training script not yet implemented (only stage configuration methods exist)

evidence: Research stage provides comprehensive literature review with 5 identified gaps, complexity table, and falsification criteria
Architecture document includes full ASCII diagram, traceability table, and risk flags
All code files have thorough docstrings describing shape conventions and design rationale
Results can be generated via consistent command-line interfaces
Validation infrastructure covers 5 universal layers with domain-specific adaptations
concerns: No actual experiment results to report in a paper
No training script (train.py) that produces trained model checkpoints
No real data loader integration means all numbers are from untrained random models
Clinician-interpretability claims are untested
Fairness claims require demographic data that may not be available

blocking_gaps: No real clinical data integration (DAIC-WOZ/E-DAIC data loaders missing)
No training script (train.py) for two-stage training pipeline
Benchmarks run on synthetic data only — no actual model has been trained
Clinician interpretability study cannot proceed without clinical partners
Fairness evaluation cannot proceed without demographic labels
Early fusion baseline not yet implemented
recommended_next_experiments: 1. Integrate DAIC-WOZ/E-DAIC data loaders and train the model from scratch
2. Run all 10 ablations with a trained model to measure real effect sizes
3. Implement MDD-MARF baseline and compare against CM-GEF on clinical test splits
4. Run missing modality stress test with 6 dropout conditions on clinical data
5. Compute demographic fairness metrics on E-DAIC (which has gender labels)
6. Measure sustained affect F1 by training a probe classifier on session features
7. Run label-efficiency sweep (5/10/25/50/100% of DAIC-WOZ) to validate LoRA hypothesis
8. Implement streaming-mode latency benchmark for real-time deployment viability
